channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Debt Hits a Concerning Milestone, and Experts Say Trump’s Policies Could Worsen It
item.description: The debt is outgrowing the size of America’s economy. The president’s policies could accelerate the country’s fiscal headaches, experts say, unless policymakers intervene.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The reaction to the U.S. debt has appeared muted on Capitol Hill, despite years of hand-wringing among Republicans, in particular, about the need for fiscal restraint.

item.title: Why Stocks and Bonds Are Responding Differently to the Iran War
item.description: Stock investors are betting that companies will make enormous profits, despite the war. But investors in bonds, including U.S. Treasuries, have other concerns.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Sommer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People attending a pro-government demonstration in Tehran last month. The S&P 500 has hit new highs since the start of the war in Iran.

item.title: House Republican Proposes Bill to Wind Down the Iran War
item.description: Representative Tom Barrett, a Michigan Republican facing a tough re-election race, introduced a bill to impose limits on the use of military force in Iran and end the fighting this summer.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Alfield Reeves for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Tom Barrett at a rally in 2024.

item.title: Trump Panel Recommends FEMA Respond to Fewer Disasters
item.description: A White House task force also called for speeding up aid, but some of its ideas would require action by Congress to become reality.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance
item.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
item.media:description: A FEMA staging area in Kerrville, Texas, last July, in the wake of the catastrophic flooding that destroyed an all-girls camp.

item.title: Trump Administration Weighs Citing ‘Emergency’ to Admit More White South Africans as Refugees
item.description: The move would further solidify the U.S. refugee program as a pipeline for the white minority from South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Hamed Aleaziz and Miriam Jordan
item.media:description: White South Africans arriving in Virginia last year after having been granted refugee status by the Trump administration.

item.title: Federal and State Officials Discuss Closing Florida’s ‘Alligator Alcatraz’
item.description: The immigrant detention center in the Everglades, which Gov. Ron DeSantis, a Republican, opened last July, may be too expensive to keep operating.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei and Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:description: President Trump and Kristi Noem, the homeland security secretary at the time, toured the immigration center known as Alligator Alcatraz with Gov. Ron DeSantis of Florida and James Uthmeier, the state’s attorney general, last July.

item.title: What My Father’s Experience Taught Me About Memory and the Brain
item.description: In the final stages of his dementia, a long-lost memory from childhood returned, perfectly formed. What was going on in his brain?
item.dc:creator: Ellen Barry and Graham Dickie
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times

item.title: Tennessee Approves New Map Aimed at Flipping the Last Democratic Seat
item.description: After a Supreme Court ruling that weakened the Voting Rights Act of 1965, Republicans carved up a majority-Black Memphis seat as the national redistricting wars continue.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Brad J. Vest for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Steve Cohen, left, Tennessee State Senator Raumesh Akbari, center, and Tennessee State Senator London Lamar, right, discuss the new House map unveiled by Tennessee Republicans on Wednesday.

item.title: Graham Platner and Susan Collins Duel in High-Stakes Maine Senate Race Ads
item.description: In strikingly different ads, Graham Platner argued that “Susan Collins’s charade is over” while Senator Collins highlighted the economic benefits she has brought to Maine.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer
item.media:description: The race in Maine between Graham Platner, a Democrat, and Senator Susan Collins, a Republican, could decide control of the Senate.

item.title: In L.A. Mayor’s Race, Karen Bass Faces Vocal Rivals and a ‘Disgruntled Electorate’
item.description: The mayor of Los Angeles is running for a second term as a former ally and a reality TV star tap into widespread voter angst.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler, Jill Cowan and Conor Dougherty
item.media:description: Mayor Karen Bass never fully recovered from her absence from California when Pacific Palisades erupted in flames last year.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein’s Purported Suicide Note Is Released by Federal Judge
item.description: A federal judge released the note, which Jeffrey Epstein’s former cellmate said he found in a graphic novel. The New York Times has not authenticated that Mr. Epstein wrote it.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser, Jan Ransom and Steve Eder

item.title: The Empty Nesters Opting for R.V. Life
item.description: With little tethering them to home, some empty nesters are taking to the road.
item.dc:creator: Tara Loader Wilkinson
item.media:credit: Whitney Curtis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Beverly Brown has been living out of her camper van since 2019. “It’s my dream van now,” she said.

item.title: Health Officials Race to Track Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak but Predict ‘Limited’ Spread
item.description: A Dutch flight attendant was among the latest to be tested in connection with a deadly outbreak on a cruise ship. One infected passenger had briefly boarded a plane to the Netherlands before she died.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses and Aimee Ortiz
item.media:description: An ambulance during an evacuation of suspected hantavirus patients, in Praia, Cape Verde, on Wednesday.

item.title: Hantavirus Response Shows How Trump Cuts Have Compromised U.S. Preparedness
item.description: The Trump administration has slashed funding for infectious disease research and has far fewer employees, including disease detectives, to respond to outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: An ambulance crew awaiting an evacuated passenger with a suspected hantavirus infection in Amsterdam on Wednesday. The U.S. government has released very little information about the outbreak on a cruise ship, which has affected several Americans.

item.title: Should You Worry About Hantavirus?
item.description: Here’s what to know about the virus, how it spreads and the risk to the general public.
item.dc:creator: Nina Agrawal and Dani Blum

item.title: About Half of Patients with Metastatic Lung Cancer Don’t Get Treatment, Study Finds
item.description: New drugs have made America’s deadliest cancer more treatable than ever. So why aren’t they reaching patients?
item.dc:creator: Simar Bajaj

item.title: Employees With Medical Conditions Challenge C.D.C. In-Office Requirement
item.description: The health agency has been particularly strict in abolishing at-home work, overriding accommodations that were granted years before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Katie Schenk, an infectious disease epidemiologist, was hired as a remote C.D.C. employee in 2024 and lost her telework accommodation. After trying in vain to get it back she quit the agency in frustration in February.

item.title: More People Are Getting Health Advice From TikTok and Instagram. Is That a Good Thing?
item.description: Half of U.S. adults under 50 now get wellness advice from influencers. A new report examines their qualifications.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Rosenbluth

item.title: New York’s $268 Billion Budget Deal Includes New Second-Home Tax
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul announced a handshake budget deal with state lawmakers that also seeks to push back on federal immigration enforcement in the state.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford and Benjamin Oreskes
item.media:credit: Mimi d’Autremont for The New York Times
item.media:description: The state budget agreement announced by Gov. Kathy Hochul on Thursday came more than five weeks after the April 1 deadline.

item.title: New York Bars ICE Agents From Wearing Masks in Broad Immigration Deal
item.description: State and local officials will also be prohibited from formally cooperating with Immigrations and Customs Enforcement under the agreement.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford
item.media:description: Masked federal agents have been a frequent presence outside immigration courtrooms in New York City.

item.title: New NTSB Report Into Deadly China Eastern Crash Suggests Struggle in Cockpit
item.description: A report by the U.S. National Transportation Safety Board offers new details about the China Eastern Airlines crash in 2022, which killed all 132 people on board.
item.dc:creator: James Glanz
item.media:description: Emergency workers searching for the black box at the crash site of the China Eastern flight in Teng County, China in 2022.

item.title: China Sentences 2 Former Defense Ministers on Bribery Charges
item.description: Gen. Wei Fenghe and Gen. Li Shangfu are likely to spend the rest of their lives in prison after receiving suspended death sentences.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:credit: Roslan Rahman/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images; Caroline Chia/Reuters

item.title: GameStop’s CEO Ryan Cohen Baffles Fans With eBay Bid
item.description: Investors questioned Ryan Cohen’s latest gambit after he gave an evasive CNBC interview and a once-loyal supporter sold his GameStop shares.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch

item.title: Is the Blake Lively v. Justin Baldoni Legal Fight Over? Not So Fast.
item.description: Lawyers for Ms. Lively and Mr. Baldoni have said the two sides settled much of the contentious dispute. But one major disagreement remains.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:description: Despite the joint statement they put out, Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni have not settled a major issue that divides them: Is she due damages in a related legal matter?

item.title: Why So Few Babies? We Might Have Overlooked the Biggest Reason of All.
item.description: We can’t be certain — and that’s the point.
item.dc:creator: Anna Louie Sussman
item.media:credit: Em Wall for The New York Times

item.title: A Legendary Investor on How to Prevent America’s Coming ‘Heart Attack’
item.description: Why Ray Dalio is bearish on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat, Emily Holzknecht and Sophia Alvarez Boyd

item.title: Meditating or Rebooting? A Robot Buddhist Monk Comes to South Korea.
item.description: Gabi, the newest monk at a temple in Seoul that is looking to spread Buddhism, vowed to not overcharge as it seeks enlightenment.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin and John Yoon
item.media:credit: Yonhap News Agency, via via Reuters

item.title: Surprising Signs of an Atmosphere Around a Tiny World, Billions of Miles Away
item.description: A gradual dimming and brightening when a star passed behind it suggested the mini-Pluto was wrapped in a thin layer of air.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

item.title: Five Ways A.I. Search Beats an Old-School Google Search
item.description: Google’s A.I. search technology is far from perfect (don’t count on it for celebrity news), but it excels at tasks like picking out groceries and detecting scams.
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen
